title: calendula incana subsp. algarbiensis
intro: Calendula incana subsp. algarbiensis is a subspecies of the annual herb Calendula incana, which belongs to the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant is also known as the Spanish marigold or Algarve marigold, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Calendula incana subsp. algarbiensis include wound healing, skin protection, and immune system support, making it a popular choice for treating minor skin irritations and injuries. Calendula incana subsp. algarbiensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or topical cream, and it is also used in folk remedies and traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
